items.title: 'Scary': Farmers and fishers face uncertain future as costs soar

children.content: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers selling in Canberra are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

gallery.altText: A fish is held up with tongs.

thumbnail.altText: A fish is held up with tongs.

items.title: Community seeks to become sole managers of ACT national park in two years

children.content: The Wreck Bay Aboriginal Community Council  plans to take on sole management of Booderee National Park In May 2028.

image.altText: A group of Indigenous dancers performed at Green Patch

items.title: Domestic violence hotline can only answer 50 per cent of calls

children.content: More than $290 million in federal funding is on the table for domestic violence services nationwide, but Canberra providers are concerned about how it will be distributed and tracked by the ACT government.

image.altText: A person with glasses and a bow tie sits in an office.

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant factor' in Canberra teen's drowning death

children.content: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

image.altText: A pond with two road bridges going over it.

items.title: 'Running on the smell of an oily rag': Community fights to open respite centre

children.content: A Queanbeyan family's plan for age-appropriate respite care is nearing completion, but without ongoing funding to secure a provider, the centre may never open its doors.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith display at War Memorial altered following arrest

children.content: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

image.altText: The newly altered display on Ben Roberts-Smith

items.title: High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

image.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

items.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

image.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

items.title: Trump's 'AI Jesus' post sparks backlash as pope responds to attack

children.content: Donald Trump is criticised by religious sections of his supporter base after publishing, then deleting, the image, but the US president says: "I thought it was me as a doctor."

image.altText: A painting-style image showing Donald Trump in white robes resting his hand on the forehead of a hospital patient.

items.title: Beijing is shifting its Taiwan strategy. Here is why

children.content: It began with a dish once served to Nixon in 1971. Now Beijing is using the same stage to reshape the Taiwan question. China is advancing a slower strategy: using economic incentives and political engagement to influence Taiwan's future.

image.altText: A close up of a Chinese man's face

items.title: Fiji Police are circling an Australian wanted over four-tonne meth import

children.content: Key insiders from Fiji's largest-ever meth bust say the drug import had alarming links to organised crime in Australia.

image.altText: A man in a blue singlet and a baseball cap drinking a coconut through a straw with gym equipment behind him.

items.title: Why Gout's incredible run deserves equal measures of praise and patience

children.content: Gout Gout was on the lips of every athletics fan across the globe on the weekend, but elements of his record-breaking run need to be treated with caution.

gallery.altText: A runner makes a shushing gesture with his finger towards the camera.
A runner strides ahead of his competitors mid-race.
Sprinter Gout Gout beats his chest and screams with joy

A runner strides ahead of his competitors mid-race.

items.title: Stocks rally, oil prices retreat on hopes of US-Iran deal

children.content: As the US Navy blockade of Iranian ports begins, stocks rise and oil prices retreat. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Iranian flag overlayed with a rising stock graph and 3D printed oil barrel miniature

items.title: Banks raise 300 red flags in five months on illicit tobacco activity

children.content: Australia's big banks have made hundreds of reports of suspicious activity related to illegal tobacco since the anti-money laundering regulator called for a crackdown late last year.

image.altText: Boxes of cigarettes surrounded by Australian cash.

items.title: Roblox to launch accounts for children after grooming claims

children.content: Roblox will launch restricted accounts for children and teenagers, two months after being put on notice by the government over reports children were being groomed on the platform.

image.altText: A boy poses for a photo while holding a smartphone in front of a screen.

items.title: Trail mix and water help 87yo woman survive near-freezing night in dense forest

children.content: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

image.altText: A forest of giant karri trees, looking upwards through the trunks to the canopy

items.title: How these queer couples split the mental load might surprise you

children.content: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

gallery.altText: Rossco and Antonio on the beach at sunset
Georgia, left, and Zoe hugging in the garden

thumbnail.altText: Rossco and Antonio on the beach at sunset
Georgia, left, and Zoe hugging in the garden

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: The neuroscience behind synaesthesia paints a surprising inner world

children.content: Have you ever tasted a word, or seen colours while listening to music? If you have, you may be among the 1 to 4 per cent of people who have a fascinating trait known as synaesthesia.

image.altText: Woman in headphones looks at bright spectrum of lights

items.title: Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds create comedy chaos in new show

children.content: Plus Nicole Kidman is an Aussie wrestler in Margo's Got Money Troubles, Baby Reindeer's Richard Gadd returns, and the reboots keep on coming with Malcolm in the Middle.

image.altText: A woman in a red and blue costume leans on the bars of a wrestling rink

items.title: Starc honoured as Wisden slams England's 'feckless' Ashes

children.content: Australia's Ashes ace Mitchell Starc is named the leading men's cricketer of the year by the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack after taking 55 wickets in 2025.

gallery.altText: Australian fast bowler Mitchell Starc turns and smiles as he raises his arm in celebration after taking an Ashes wicket.

thumbnail.altText: Australian fast bowler Mitchell Starc turns and smiles as he raises his arm in celebration after taking an Ashes wicket.

items.title: Collard to learn sanction after being found guilty of homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard will learn via an AFL hearing how heavy a suspension he will receive after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur towards a VFL opponent.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL player holds a football in one hand in a warm-up before a game.

items.title: Terminally ill MP says ban on telehealth for assisted dying must change

children.content: A Labor politician living with MND writes to the attorney-general, calling for laws banning doctors from discussing voluntary assisted dying during telehealth appointments to be revised.

image.altText: Woman in read seated on set of stairs wearing a blue beanie.

items.title: Bomb squad called in after rental home cleaner finds explosives, drugs, cash

children.content: Authorities were forced to conduct controlled explosions at a property in Melbourne's north-west after a cleaner stumbled across drugs and explosives.

image.altText: Police vehicles.

items.title: Teenager 'begging' minister to deport abuser who had visa reinstated

children.content: A girl who was sexually assaulted in Perth by a UK citizen when she was nine years old writes to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, pleading with him to cancel the man's visa after he avoided deportation on appeal.

image.altText: Tony Burke wears grey suit and is speaking at a press conference.

items.title: How Sydney's underworld is breaking a once unspoken rule

children.content: Sydney's underworld used to operate by a code, but those unspoken rules have come undone. In today's gangland wars, violence is spilling into suburbia, and anyone can become collateral damage.

gallery.altText: The faces of five victims killed in Sydney's gangland wars

thumbnail.altText: The faces of five victims killed in Sydney's gangland wars

items.title: Australia's LNG exporters will face a tougher future, new report says

children.content: The long-term outlook for Australia's liquefied natural gas exports is structurally constrained by declining demand, and the current war in the Middle East may hasten that shift away from gas, a new report claims.

image.altText: An LNG flare flames out from a stack.

items.title: Hungarian voters showed little regard for Donald Trump's wishes

children.content: A record number of Hungarians turned out not just to send a strong message to their far-right prime minister, but to reject Donald Trump.

image.altText: Viktor Orban concedes the hungarian election on stage surrounded by supporters

items.title: California Democrat Eric Swalwell to resign from US Congress

children.content: Eric Swalwell, a California Democrat, says he will resign from Congress following multiple sexual assault allegations.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a bear poster.

items.title: Southport girls' murders could have been prevented, UK inquiry finds

children.content: An inquiry into the murders of three young girls at a Taylor Swift-themed dance event in Britain finds there was a "fundamental failure" by state bodies and the killer's parents to recognise and act on the risk he posed.

image.altText: A composite image of three young girls, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Mother of Bondi Junction victim rebukes 'sympathy dressed up as action'

children.content: The bereaved family of one of the Bondi Junction stabbing victims says families like hers "do not need more sympathy dressed up as action" from the NSW government.

gallery.altText: Elizabeth Young with her dog and a photo of her daughter, Bondi Junction victim Jade Young

thumbnail.altText: Elizabeth Young with her dog and a photo of her daughter, Bondi Junction victim Jade Young

items.title: Closing the gap concerns over Aboriginal investment body's strategy

children.content: Indigenous people in the NT are being offered the chance to tap into a $680-million royalties fund, but some analysts worry the money is being spent too cautiously.

image.altText: Two men look at an artist's impression of a new building

items.title: Philippines accuses China of 'sabotage' by dumping cyanide in South China Sea

children.content: Manila has accused China of poisoning waters around a ship where it has marines stationed, but Beijing has dismissed the allegations.

image.altText: Filipino marines standing on the deck of a rusty ship.

items.title: Ukulele club's 600 members may be strumming their way to better brain health

children.content: From a small gathering to a packed RSL, this Queensland ukulele club has found its rhythm, and a brain researcher says playing could offer its members a multitude of health benefits.
